abstract_summary: AI scientist systems are described as tools, coauthors, or founders, but we evaluate them as if only the final answer matters. This position paper argues that outcome-only evaluation is insufficient, and that task outcome, mechanism fidelity, and epistemic honesty must be measured separately. Our evidence comes from...
citation: Position: Correct Answer, Wrong Mechanism -- When AI Scientists Defend General Claims Their Own Data Contradicts. Steven Young Eulig. 2026-06-22. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23175

relevance.curation_note: Useful evaluation paper because it focuses on mechanism fidelity and epistemic honesty, a key failure mode for AI-scientist systems.
relevance.reason: Studies evaluation failure modes for AI-scientist systems, especially mechanism fidelity and epistemic honesty.
